calendar.matters:
Petition To Retrieve Estate Property, For Surcharge, And To Reduce The Bond Amount
Off Calendar Jul-25-2018 Text Ruling

Petition To Retrieve Estate Property, For Surcharge, And To Reduce The Bond Amount
Off Calendar May-14-2018 Continued To Jun-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lynn Searle for Melanie Cahn (Petitioner)
Gino Molinari for Bob Cano (Respondent)
Tracy Stevenson for US Specialty Insurance via CourtCall

Ms. Searle spoke of her not filing an accounting, no objections by the beneficiaries, she has been in contact with Ms. Stevenson, that they have agreed to disagree, and that there is no order signed and filed by the court reducing the bond to zero dollars. Ms. Searle further stated her concerns re: Wells Fargo credit card debt, assets from Schwab should have been in estate, the former administrator had a duty to collect the asset and failed to do so, and whether bond premium should be charged to former administrator ($700.00/year), not charged to the estate. Mr. Molinari stated that he is still waiting for un-redacted documents he subpoenaed from Wells Fargo. Ms. Searle stated that this is Mr. Molinari's 3rd or 4th subpoena issued to Wells Fargo, that those documents are not relevant or at issue with this petition. Mr. Molinari responded that he thought they were, and inquired if Ms. Searle is not going to raise issues regarding the Wells Fargo Account. Ms. Searle responded they are not issues of checks before the decedent's death. The Petition to Retrieve Estate Property, etc. is continued to June 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trial. Trial Briefs, Exhibit/Witness Lists to be filed by June 22, 2018.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Retrieve Estate Property, For Surcharge, And To Reduce The Bond Amount
Off Calendar May-07-2018 Continued To May-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Retrieve Estate Property, For Surcharge, And To Reduce The Bond Amount
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Continued To May-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gino Molinari for Robert Cano; Lynn Searle for petitioner Melanie Cain; and Tristan Espinosa (by CourtCall) for U.S. Specialty Insurance.

The Court stated that the matter was continued to today in order to give the parties time to make additional submissions, but that no such supplemental filings had been received by the court. He inquired regarding the current status of the case. Mr. Molinari stated that some discovery had been served, and that some responses to discovery were not yet due, so discovery was incomplete. Mr. Espinose said that his client was seeking production of credit card and Wells Fargo Bank statements. Mr. Molinari requested that the Court issue an order directing the bank to not redact statements before producing them. Ms. Searle reported that Mr. Molinari has failed to produce his files or files of Mr. Cano as previously ordered by the Court. Mr. Molinari stated that when the decedent passed away his caregivers went to the bank and withdrew funds, and he needs to get documentation regarding such transactions from the bank. Ms. Searle stated that discovery was not yet complete, so the matter was not yet ready for the setting of a date for an evidentiary hearing. The Court inquired how much additional time was needed. Ms. Searle suggested 60 days would be sufficient, and Mr. Espinosa agreed. The matter was continued to May 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status and setting of the evidentiary hearing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Shirley Le. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported) Advanced

Petition To Retrieve Estate Property, For Surcharge, And To Reduce The Bond Amount
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lynn Searle for Melanie Cahn.
Gino Molinari for Robert Cano.
Tracy Stevenson appeared by telephone for U.S. Specialty Insurance Company.

Ms. Searle stated that she believes she filed her petition under 850 for return of property and for damages under 859 for Mr. Cano's liability for breach of fiduciary duty and the assurity for liability on bond. The Court raised the issue of setting the matter for an evidentiary hearing on the surcharge. Mr. Molinari stated that his Client will not be available for two and a half months. Ms. Stevenson stated that her Client is entitled to any evidence Mr. Cano can submit and that the fight is about $9,000.00. The Parties indicated that they need to conduct some discovery. The Petition to Retrieve Estate Property and for Surcharge is continued to March 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery and for trial setting.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Retrieve Estate Property, For Surcharge, And To Reduce The Bond Amount
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Continued To Feb-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lynne Searle for Melanie Cahn (Petitioner).
Gino Molinari for Bob Cano (Respondent).
Tracy Stevenson appeared by telephone for US Specialty.

Mr. Molinari stated that he filed an opposition to the surcharge request today, that he is trying to gather evidence to show that the $9,500.00 has been spent on the Estate and that the alleged acts were done way before Mr. Cano was appointed. Ms. Searle stated that the petition was filed at the request of the Court, that in 2010 by way of a 13100 Mr. Cano removed the assets from Wells Fargo Bank and that he filed a fraudulent inventory of $150,000.00 when he knew he had already taken those assets. Ms. Stevenson stated that Mr. Cano can not be charged for anything that was properly spent on the Estate and that she would like to see an accounting of the $9,000.00. Mr. Molinari stated that $2,500.00 was taken by Wells Fargo and that he needs to subpoena Wells Fargo. Ms. Searle raised the issue of who was using the Decedent's credit card and who made the charges, stated that it is her understanding that Wells Fargo took that money for the credit card charges and that prior to death, there was about $20,000.00. Mr. Molinari stated that it was taken by the care providers. The Petition to Retrieve Estate Property, etc. is continued to February 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of something showing how funds were spent. Ms. Searle raised the issue of bond, stated that her Client has been unable to pay for the bond, that there is no money and requested that the Court reduce bond to zero. The Court granted Ms. Searle's request and noted that no one is opposing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-22-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-07-294307 Estate of Albert J. Lococo and PES-13-297123 Estate of Joseph A. Lococo.

Appearances: Gino Molinari for Robert Cano.
Lynn Searle for Melanie Cahn.
Tracy Stevenson appeared by telephone for US Specialty Insurance Company.

The Court noted that it now has a petition for surcharge in Joseph's estate with a hearing date of September 25, 2017 which has money that went out that may have benefited Albert's estate, and stated that it does not have the bank statements, that they were referenced as Exhibit-A in the status report, but they were not attached. Mr. Molinari stated that $1,195.00 was paid to S & K Accounting, that they recently found out that one of the Heir's (Lawrence) check in the amount of $3,125.00 was never cashed, and that if he can reference it in the accounting, he can rectify the outstanding balance. Ms. Searle stated that she discovered that the former Administrator listed some assets that belonged to the Decedent and some that were not, that according to Schwab in 2010 Albert's sisters collected the entire Schwab account under 13100 with directions to transfer the funds into an account in Albert's name, that the new account number does not show a name, that the inventory and appraisal on Albert's estate indicates that account number, and that the partial inventory and appraisal filed by the Former Administrator listed cash, but not the securities. Ms. Searle also stated that she does not have any proof that the Sisters acted knowing Joseph had a will, and that she sees no point in going into Albert's estate to put the funds back in Joseph's estate, just to give it back to Albert. Ms. Stevenson stated that they did not name Albert's estate, that they are not seeking to transfer assets and that they don't see any impediment. The status report petition in Albert's estate is continued to October 3, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a petition for final distribution. If Mr. Molinari is not in a position to file said petition, the Court expects an amended or a new petition to approve the status report. Status on close of estate in Joseph's estate is off calendar in favor of the now filed petition.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Former Personal Representative Turning Over Assets And Records To The New Personal Representative
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gino Molinari for (former administrator) Robert Cano. Lynne Searle for (administrator) Melanie Cahn. Amber Woo (via Court Call) for U.S. Specialty Insurance Co.

The Court asked if Mr. Cano has filed the accounting. Mr. Molinari replied no, that the issue today is the subpoena of records, that they have received records from Redwood and Schwab but only partial records from Wells Fargo Bank (WFB), that WFB says it needs more information, that they will reissue the subpoena to WFB, that they currently only have proof of an account with about $7,000.00 in it. The Court stated that Mr. Cano was ordered to file an accounting and that they were to turn over records to Ms. Cahn. Ms. Searle stated that they did not turn over the records, that they did not receive copies of the subpoena and that she was given some records this morning. Mr. Molinari stated that Ms. Searle received notice of the subpoena, that she could have gotten a copy by calling his office, that he thought she had the records and that there is a pending surcharge. Ms. Searle stated that she has not filed the petition yet but plans to do so shortly, that she has been busy and will be leaving the country for a week and will file it when she returns. Mr. Molinari stated that this activity with the banks was done in 2010, that Mr. Cano was not appointed administrator until December 2013, that the funds had been distributed to Al Lococo and then transferred into his estate, that they are waiting for the WFB records and that he has records to give to the Court. The Court stated that it intends to take the status on filing an accounting and status on turning over documents off calendar. Ms. Searle stated that there is one more issue, that her client was appointed with a fee waiver, that they did not know that the assets were missing, that a bond was ordered in the amount of $120,000.00 and is still outstanding, that the premium of $500.00 has not been paid, that they want the bond exonerated back to the date ordered but would like the letters to stay valid. The Court stated that it will take the bond issue under submission and that the status on filing a first account and report and the status on turning over documents and assets to Ms. Cahn are off calendar. Ms. Woo stated that she joins in Mr. Molinari's request for a continuance to re issue the subpoena. The Court stated that Ms. Searle will file her petition. Ms. Searle asked if the Court could order Mr. Cano to pay the bond and the Court responded that it cannot.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Former Personal Representative Turning Over Assets And Records To The New Personal Representative
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Continued To Oct-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lynn Searle for Melanie Cahn. Gino Molinari for and with (former executor) Robert Cano. Tracy Stevenson (via court call) for (interested party) U.S. Specialty Insurance.

The Court asked if there has been progress. Ms. Searle stated that they have not received an accounting, that they have received a large file of records of the decedent but not of the probate, that they would like a surcharge hearing and will need 45 days to file the petition. Mr. Molinari stated that they understand that all assets and files were delivered on April 6, 2016, that one setback has been of obtaining the records from the various banks, that they failed to send the necessary form but have now done so, that the documents are due to them on August 25, 2016 and that these documents will assist with the accounting. Ms. Stevenson stated that she concurs, but added that the banks never produce the records on the date they are supposed to and that it could be two weeks later than the August 25th date. Ms. Searle stated that they do not know what subpoena's were sent or what records he is looking for and that the statements delivered to her are missing some months. Mr. Molinari stated that they are in the process of obtaining documents from Redwood Empire, Wells Fargo and Charles Schwab and that once they get the records they will be able to do the accounting. The Court continued the status on former personal representative turning over assets and records to the new personal representative and the status of filing the first account and report to October 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow time for Ms. Searle to file her petition and for Mr. Molinari to obtain the documents and file the accounting.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Former Personal Representative Turning Over Assets And Records To The New Personal Representative
Off Calendar May-09-2016 Continued To Jul-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gino Molinari & James Carter for Robert Cano. Lynne Searle for Melanie Cahn. Tracy Stevenson (via court call) for the Surety company.

The Court asked if Ms. Searle plans to file a 1310(b) petition. Ms. Searle stated that she has the petition ready to file and hopes to get the fee issue resolved. The Court stated that they will need to submit a new request. Mr. Molinari stated that there has been confusion, gave a chronological overview of this case and the companion case, that Albert left his entire estate to Joseph, that Joseph predeceased Albert which would cause Albert's estate to be split between the Molinari and Lococo families, that Melanie is not part of either family, that the executor for Albert's estate is Lucille Molinari, that Robert Cano does not have the capability to return the funds, that the two sisters transferred $116,000.00 in funds to Albert, that Mr. Cano had nothing to do with this, that upon Albert's death it went into his estate, that Mr. Cano was not administering the estate, that Mr. Cano did not request preliminary distribution or fees, that he has no control of the assets, that the sisters are not before this court, that Lucille lives in Montana and is not before the court, requests that the court set aside the 2/22 order and set a date for a long course hearing, that they will withdraw their appeal and that Mr. Cano had nothing to do with this. Ms. Searle stated that she has nothing further. Ms. Stevenson stated that she just received the file last week, that an 850 petition should be filed to recover the assets and that she would like to see documentation that the sisters transferred the funds. Ms. Searle stated that she would like that documentation as well. Mr. Molinari stated that they need time, that his file contains both estates and that he will need 45 days. The Court stated that the informal request to reconsider the order will not be acted on. Ms. Searle stated that she will be filing her petition ex parte. The status on former personal representative turning over assets and records to new personal representative and the status re proof of payment of fees were continued to July 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Former Personal Representative Turning Over Assets And Records To The New Personal Representative
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Continued To May-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lynne Searle for (administrator) Melanie Cahn. Gino Molinari for and with (former administrator) Robert Cano. James Carter also present.

The Court asked for status on the receipt of documents. Mr. Molinari stated that they finally have the accounts and handed a copy to Ms. Searle. Ms. Searle stated that she has received a partial file, that it contains financial records dated pre-death and date of death but nothing post death. Mr. Molinari stated that he just handed her the post death documents today, that they were ordered to prepare a first and final accounting but are confused and need an additional 30 days. Ms. Searle stated that she had advised Mr. Molinari of the due dates, that she emailed a copy of the order and drew attention to the dates, that she also advised Mr. Carter of the dates, that the document that he just handed to her looks like a bank statement but does not include information on the Schwab, Redwood or Wells Fargo accounts and that she believes that the money was withdrawn from the Redwood and Wells Fargo accounts in July 2010. The Court asked how much money is currently in the accounts. Mr. Molinari stated zero, that it was transferred to the father's estate. The Court stated that that case number is 294307 and asked when the money would be returned. Mr. Molinari stated that the accounts were transferred to Al. Ms. Searle stated that those assets belonged to the son, that they were not pay on death accounts, that she has been advised that they were claimed with a 13100, that the Schwab funds are not accounted for, that they found letters to banks looking for funds but no return letters from the banks except 1 that said there were not funds and that the assets listed on the inventory in Dad's estate look like the funds that belonged to Joe. The Court stated that it will set a 10 day status date to transfer funds back in the full amount and that Ms. Searle is to prepare a proposed order regarding what is outstanding. Ms. Searle stated that the Inventory & Appraisal in the matter shows a total of $113,000.00 but if you total it up it is actually $119,000.00. The Court stated that the proposed order should include and estate documents not yet received and the orders from today. Ms. Searle stated that they want all post date of death records turned over, that they are considering filing a petition for surcharge and would like the fee waiver reinstated. Mr. Molinari stated that the petitioner received a substantial inheritance last year and should pay the fees. The status on former personal representative turning over asses and records to the new personal representative and the status regarding proof of payment of fees were continued to May 2, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for proof of transfer.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gino Molinari & James Carter for Robert Cano. Lynn Searle for and with Melanie Cano.

The Court asked the parties to address the first will and then the second. Mr. Molinari stated that the wills are not holographic, that they feel 6124 applies, that the testator was in possession of the will and was competent and that they request an offer of proof that the wills were destroyed by someone other than the testator. Ms. Searle stated that it is presumption only, that the amount of evidence required is slight, that it casts doubt on the presumption, that the will was written shortly before death, that he attempted to revise it, that his physical capacity was compromised and was unable to hold a pen, that they doubt he destroyed the will, that the statute requires a physical act that he was incapable of performing, that Mr. Molinari is not contesting that the 3/17 document intended to be the will, that the burden is on Mr. Molinari's client to prove, that in regards to the 4/24 documents it is the same document not a separate document, that the question is if the alterations were done at his direction, that a hearing would be necessary for this and that there is not a second signature. The Court asked about removal. Ms. Searle stated as directed by the statute. Mr. Molinari reported that Mr. Cano knew that the other two wills were not original, that the delays were due to the trial and the appeal and that the only fraud issue is that the petition filed a fee waiver but has received $100,000.00 from the lower court. Mr. Carter stated that he was present at the trial, that Ms. Cahn received $130,000.00 and is now trying to get the rest and that they are relying on documents that do not stand up. Mr. Molinari stated that he agrees and that the will was not witnessed or signed by the testator. Ms. Searle stated that the appellate court determined that there was sufficient evidence. Mr. Molinari stated that the issue is not validity, that the issue is the transfer of beneficiaries on the IRA or CD and that the will issue was not decided. The Court stated that it will grant admission of the 3/17/10 will, deny admission of the 4/24/10 will and remove Mr. Cano. The Court addressed the issue of the repayment of the fee waiver. Ms. Searle stated that her client is on SSI, that she does not have the money yet and that it may have to go into a special needs trust. The Court set a status for May 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on payment of the previously waived fees, that if fees have not been paid at that time counsel should file a statement regarding the ability to pay the fees.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
